<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Symplectic manifolds</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

Symplectic manifolds

A symplectic manifold is a smooth even-dimensional space carrying a closed non-degenerate 2-form $\omega$. That single piece of structure encodes all of Hamiltonian mechanics: vector fields from energy functions, conservation laws, canonical transformations, and — through Floer's loop-space construction — a hands-on homology theory whose ranks bound the periodic orbits of any Hamiltonian.

blocks.html:
1. Symplectic form

Fix a smooth manifold $M$. A symplectic form on $M$ is a 2-form $\omega\in\Omega^2(M)$ satisfying two conditions:

  • Closed: $d\omega = 0$.
  • Non-degenerate: for every $p\in M$, the bilinear form $\omega_p\colon T_pM\times T_pM\to\mathbb{R}$ is non-degenerate. Equivalently, $v\mapsto \iota_v\omega_p$ is an isomorphism $T_pM\to T_p^*M$.

Non-degeneracy of an alternating bilinear form forces even dimension: an antisymmetric matrix in odd size has zero determinant. So $\dim M = 2n$, and the top exterior power $\omega^n = \omega\wedge\cdots\wedge\omega$ is a nowhere-vanishing $2n$-form — a canonical volume form. In particular every symplectic manifold is orientable.

Examples to keep in mind.
  • $\mathbb{R}^{2n}$ with $\omega_0 = \sum_i dp_i\wedge dq_i$ (the model).
  • The cotangent bundle $T^*N$ with $\omega = -d\lambda$, where $\lambda = \sum p_i\,dq_i$ is the Liouville 1-form. This is the symplectic stage of classical mechanics on configuration space $N$.
  • $S^2$ with its area form. Any orientable surface is symplectic.
  • $\mathbb{C}P^n$ with the Fubini–Study form (it's Kähler).
  • Counterexample: $S^4$ admits no symplectic form (any closed $\omega$ on $S^4$ is exact, and exact forms can't have non-vanishing $\omega^2$).

Widget · alternating vs symmetric pairingsdrag two vectors; watch $\omega(u,v)$ sweep the signed area

Drag the heads of $u$ (yellow) and $v$ (cyan). Symplectic mode shades the parallelogram by signed oriented area $\omega(u,v) = u_xv_y - u_yv_x$ — flip $u\leftrightarrow v$ and the sign flips. Metric mode shows $g(u,v) = u_xv_x+u_yv_y$, a symmetric pairing without orientation.

See also
  • Exterior derivative
  • Differential forms and wedge product
  • Smooth manifolds and atlases

Used in

Symplectic manifolds · Hamiltonian vector fields and flows
Symplectic manifolds · Lagrangian submanifolds

2. Darboux's theorem

The first thing to ask about any geometric structure is: what are its local invariants? For Riemannian metrics it's curvature; for complex structures it's nothing in the integrable case (Newlander–Nirenberg) but plenty in general. For symplectic forms the answer is nothing at all.

Darboux's theorem. Let $(M^{2n},\omega)$ be symplectic. Around every point $p\in M$ there is a chart $(p_1,\dots,p_n,q_1,\dots,q_n)$ with $p\mapsto 0$ in which $$\omega \;=\; \sum_{i=1}^n dp_i\wedge dq_i.$$ Equivalently, every symplectic $2n$-manifold is locally symplectomorphic to $(\mathbb{R}^{2n},\omega_0)$.

The proof is a Moser-trick deformation. Pick a chart taking $\omega_p$ to $\omega_0$ at the origin (linear algebra: any non-degenerate alternating form on $\mathbb{R}^{2n}$ is $\omega_0$ in suitable coordinates). Define $\omega_t = (1-t)\omega_0 + t\omega$ for $t\in[0,1]$. Both endpoints are symplectic at the origin; by closedness write $\omega - \omega_0 = d\alpha$ on a small ball, then solve $\iota_{X_t}\omega_t = -\alpha$ for a time-dependent vector field $X_t$. Its flow is a diffeomorphism $\Phi$ with $\Phi^*\omega = \omega_0$. Done.

Widget · Moser deformation $\omega_0\to\omega$drag the time slider — the same coordinates support every intermediate symplectic form
$t = 0$

A grid of unit symplectic cells deforms with $t$: at $t=0$ each cell carries the standard area $1$; at $t=1$ a stretched/sheared form distributes the same total area. Darboux says a single chart change relates the two — there is no local invariant to obstruct it.

See also
  • Differential forms · the wedge product and $\Lambda^2 T^*M$.
  • Differential forms · the exterior derivative and $d^2 = 0$.
  • Smooth manifolds · tangent spaces.

3. Hamiltonian vector fields and flows

Non-degeneracy of $\omega$ is the bridge from functions to dynamics. Given a smooth $H\colon M\to\mathbb{R}$, its differential $dH$ is a 1-form. Apply the inverse of $v\mapsto\iota_v\omega$:

Definition. The Hamiltonian vector field $X_H$ associated to $H$ is the unique vector field satisfying $$\iota_{X_H}\,\omega \;=\; dH.$$

In Darboux coordinates $\omega = \sum dp_i\wedge dq_i$, the equation unwraps to Hamilton's equations: $$\dot q_i \;=\; \tfrac{\partial H}{\partial p_i}, \qquad \dot p_i \;=\; -\tfrac{\partial H}{\partial q_i}.$$ Two facts come for free:

  • Energy conservation. $\dot H = X_H(H) = dH(X_H) = \omega(X_H,X_H) = 0$ by antisymmetry. $H$ is constant along trajectories.
  • Symplectic flow. By Cartan's formula $\mathcal{L}_{X_H}\omega = d\iota_{X_H}\omega + \iota_{X_H}d\omega = d(dH) + 0 = 0$. So the time-$t$ flow $\Phi_t^{X_H}$ is a symplectomorphism — Liouville's theorem.

Widget · phase portrait of the harmonic oscillatordrag the energy slider; watch level sets stack into ellipses
$E = 1.0$

For $H = \tfrac12(p^2+q^2)$ the level set $\{H=E\}$ is the circle of radius $\sqrt{2E}$. The Hamiltonian flow is uniform rotation: $(p,q)\mapsto(p\cos t - q\sin t,\,p\sin t + q\cos t)$. The dot orbits one level circle while the gradient field (light ticks) shows $X_H = (-q,p)$ everywhere tangent to circles — never radial.

See also
  • Smooth manifolds · integral curves and flows of vector fields.

Used in
Symplectic manifolds · Floer homology — a preview
Symplectic manifolds · Poisson bracket

4. Poisson bracket

Hamiltonian vector fields close under the Lie bracket, but the right way to see this is via a bracket on functions.

Definition. The Poisson bracket of $f,g\in C^\infty(M)$ is $$\{f,g\} \;=\; \omega(X_f, X_g) \;=\; X_f(g) \;=\; -X_g(f).$$

In Darboux coordinates this is $\{f,g\} = \sum_i \big(\tfrac{\partial f}{\partial p_i}\tfrac{\partial g}{\partial q_i} - \tfrac{\partial f}{\partial q_i}\tfrac{\partial g}{\partial p_i}\big)$ — the formula from classical mechanics textbooks. Three structural identities:

  • Antisymmetry: $\{f,g\} = -\{g,f\}$.
  • Leibniz: $\{f,gh\} = \{f,g\}h + g\{f,h\}$.
  • Jacobi: $\{f,\{g,h\}\} + \{g,\{h,f\}\} + \{h,\{f,g\}\} = 0$.

Antisymmetry is automatic; Leibniz follows because $X_f$ is a derivation; Jacobi is the deepest — it depends on $d\omega = 0$. So $C^\infty(M)$ becomes a Poisson algebra (commutative under pointwise product, Lie under $\{\cdot,\cdot\}$, with the two operations linked by Leibniz).

Quantisation. Dirac's correspondence sends $f\mapsto \hat f$ with $$\{f,g\} \;\leadsto\; \frac{[\hat f,\hat g]}{i\hbar}.$$ The quantum commutator is the classical bracket plus $O(\hbar)$ corrections. This is why classical mechanics is the limit of quantum mechanics — the algebraic structures are the same, with $\hbar\to 0$ flattening the operators back to functions.

Widget · classical / quantum correspondence ladderclick a row to expand the matching pair
click a pair

Each row pairs a classical observable (left) with its quantum counterpart (right). The dashed bridge is $\{\cdot,\cdot\}\leadsto [\cdot,\cdot]/(i\hbar)$. Click to highlight: position-momentum gives $\{q,p\} = 1\leadsto [\hat q,\hat p] = i\hbar$; angular-momentum components give $\{L_x,L_y\} = L_z\leadsto [\hat L_x,\hat L_y] = i\hbar\hat L_z$.

5. Lagrangian submanifolds

The right "subobjects" of a symplectic manifold are not symplectic submanifolds (those exist but are rare and rigid) but Lagrangian submanifolds — half-dimensional and totally isotropic.

Definition. A submanifold $L\subset (M^{2n},\omega)$ is Lagrangian if $\dim L = n$ and $\omega|_L = 0$ (i.e. $\omega(u,v)=0$ for all $u,v\in T_pL$ and all $p\in L$).

Half-dimensionality is forced by maximality: an isotropic subspace of $(T_pM,\omega_p)$ has dimension at most $n$, and equality is the Lagrangian condition. Two key examples:

  1. Cotangent bundle. In $T^*N$ with canonical form $\omega = -d\lambda$, the zero section $\{(x,0):x\in N\}$ is Lagrangian. More generally the graph $\Gamma_\alpha = \{(x,\alpha_x)\}$ of a 1-form $\alpha\in\Omega^1(N)$ is Lagrangian iff $d\alpha = 0$, i.e. iff $\alpha$ is closed. So Lagrangians near the zero section correspond to closed 1-forms — the link to de Rham theory.
  2. Graphs of symplectomorphisms. Equip $M_1\times M_2$ with $\omega_1\ominus\omega_2 := \pi_1^*\omega_1 - \pi_2^*\omega_2$. The graph $\Gamma_f = \{(x,f(x))\}\subset M_1\times M_2$ is Lagrangian iff $f\colon M_1\to M_2$ is a symplectomorphism. Canonical relations (general Lagrangians of $M_1\times M_2$) compose to give a symplectic category.

Weinstein's slogan: "everything is Lagrangian." Periodic orbits are intersections with a Lagrangian; integrable systems foliate $M$ by Lagrangian tori (Liouville–Arnold); generating functions describe symplectomorphisms by the Lagrangian graph in a cotangent bundle. Mirror symmetry trades Lagrangians on one side for coherent sheaves on the other.

Widget · Lagrangians in $T^*S^1$click a closed 1-form $\alpha$ to see its graph wrap the cylinder

$T^*S^1$ is the cylinder $S^1\times\mathbb{R}$ with $\omega = d\theta\wedge dp$. Lagrangians come in two flavours: horizontal sections $\{p=c\}$ (graphs of $\alpha=c\,d\theta$, parameter $c\in\mathbb{R}$) and vertical fibers $\{\theta=\theta_0\}$. Generic non-closed forms like $f(\theta)d\theta$ with $f$ non-constant give Lagrangian graphs only when $f$ is a constant — that's the closedness obstruction.

See also
  • Smooth manifolds · immersions, submersions, and submanifolds.
  • Tangent spaces

Used in
Symplectic manifolds · Floer homology — a preview

6. Floer homology — a preview

Symplectic topology contains rigid theorems that look impossible from the differential-topology side. The cleanest example is the Arnold conjecture:

Arnold conjecture (Floer's theorem). Let $(M,\omega)$ be a closed symplectic manifold and $H_t$ a non-degenerate time-1-periodic Hamiltonian. The number of 1-periodic orbits of $X_{H_t}$ is at least $\sum_k b_k(M)$.

This is a Morse inequality on an infinite-dimensional space. Floer made it rigorous as follows.

Define the symplectic action functional on the free loop space $\mathcal{L}M$ (loops $\gamma\colon S^1\to M$): $$\mathcal{A}_H(\gamma) \;=\; -\int_{D^2}\bar u^*\omega \;-\; \int_0^1 H(t,\gamma(t))\,dt,$$ where $\bar u\colon D^2\to M$ is any extension of $\gamma$ (the multivaluedness of the first term is mild and controlled). The critical points of $\mathcal{A}_H$ are exactly the 1-periodic orbits of $X_{H_t}$. The "$L^2$-gradient" of $\mathcal{A}_H$ with respect to a compatible almost-complex structure $J$ is the Cauchy–Riemann operator: gradient flow lines from orbit $x$ to orbit $y$ are pseudo-holomorphic cylinders $$\partial_s u + J(u)(\partial_t u - X_{H_t}) \;=\; 0$$ joining $x$ at $s=-\infty$ to $y$ at $s=+\infty$. Counting them mod the obvious $\mathbb{R}$-action gives a chain complex $$\partial x \;=\; \sum_{\mu(x,y)=1} \#\widehat{\mathcal{M}}(x,y)\cdot y,$$ and Floer proved $\partial^2 = 0$ and $HF_*(M;H,J) \cong H_*(M)$.

The Lagrangian variant works between two Lagrangians $L_0,L_1\subset M$. Generators: transverse intersections $L_0\cap L_1$. Differentials: pseudo-holomorphic strips with boundary on $L_0$ and $L_1$. The resulting $HF_*(L_0,L_1)$ is the morphism space in the Fukaya category — the symplectic side of homological mirror symmetry.

Widget · two Lagrangian circles in $T^2$drag the second circle; count intersections vs Hamiltonian-isotopy lower bound
$\theta = 0.20$

On $T^2 = \mathbb{R}^2/\mathbb{Z}^2$ with the standard $\omega = dx\wedge dy$, take $L_0 = \{y = 0\}$ (red) and $L_1 = $ a Hamiltonian-isotopic deformation of the vertical circle $\{x = 0\}$ (cyan). The intersections $L_0\cap L_1$ are highlighted dots; their count is at least $|H_*(S^1)| = 2$ — the rank of the Floer chain complex. No matter how you wiggle $L_1$ by a Hamiltonian, you can't cancel both intersection points.

See also
  • Morse theory · the finite-dimensional model — Floer is its loop-space analogue.
  • Morse theory · Morse homology, the chain-complex Floer generalises.

7. Connections

Symplectic geometry sits at a crossroads. Locally trivial (Darboux) but globally rigid (Gromov non-squeezing, Floer-theoretic obstructions), it is the natural language for both classical mechanics and a swathe of modern topology — and the open problems are dense. The symplectic isotopy question, the existence of exotic symplectic structures on $\mathbb{R}^{2n}$, the Weinstein conjecture on Reeb orbits, and homological mirror symmetry are all live research frontiers.

  • Differential forms — de Rham cohomology — closedness of $\omega$ gives a class $[\omega]\in H^2(M;\mathbb{R})$; its powers detect symplectic obstructions in cohomology.
  • Smooth manifolds — integral curves and flows — Hamiltonian flows are the worked example that motivates the whole apparatus of vector-field flows.
  • Morse theory — applications — Floer homology is "Morse theory on the loop space"; the parallel is exact down to chain-complex structure.
  • Differential geometry — almost-complex structures, the Levi-Civita connection on the loop space, and the analytic estimates underlying Floer.
  • Lie groups — coadjoint orbits are symplectic (Kirillov–Kostant–Souriau), giving the geometric input to the orbit method in representation theory.

/* §1 alternating vs symmetric pairing */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("f-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const out = document.getElementById("f-out");
  const bOmega = document.getElementById("f-omega");
  const bG = document.getElementById("f-g");
  let mode = "omega";
  // anchor + two draggable vector heads
  const O = {x:380, y:170};
  const u = {x:O.x+90, y:O.y-30};
  const v = {x:O.x+30, y:O.y+90};
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "<title>Widget · symplectic form vs metric on a tangent plane</title>";
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:200,y1:O.y,x2:580,y2:O.y,stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:O.x,y1:30,x2:O.x,y2:300,stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1}));
    // parallelogram
    const P3 = {x:u.x + (v.x - O.x), y:u.y + (v.y - O.y)};
    const fill = mode === "omega" ? "rgba(125,224,214,0.18)" : "rgba(255,216,102,0.18)";
    const stroke = mode === "omega" ? "var(--cyan)" : "var(--yellow)";
    svg.appendChild(SVG("polygon",{
      points:`${O.x},${O.y} ${u.x},${u.y} ${P3.x},${P3.y} ${v.x},${v.y}`,
      fill, stroke, "stroke-width":1, "stroke-dasharray":"4 3"
    }));
    // u and v arrows
    drawArrow(svg, [O.x,O.y], [u.x,u.y], {color:"var(--yellow)", markerId:"f-u", width:2});
    drawArrow(svg, [O.x,O.y], [v.x,v.y], {color:"var(--cyan)", markerId:"f-v", width:2});
    // labels
    const lu = SVG("text",{x:u.x+10,y:u.y-8,"font-size":13,fill:"var(--yellow)","font-style":"italic"}); lu.textContent = "u"; svg.appendChild(lu);
    const lv = SVG("text",{x:v.x+10,y:v.y+14,"font-size":13,fill:"var(--cyan)","font-style":"italic"}); lv.textContent = "v"; svg.appendChild(lv);
    // draggable handles
    const hu = SVG("circle",{cx:u.x,cy:u.y,r:9,fill:"var(--yellow)",stroke:"#111","stroke-width":1.5,style:"cursor:grab"});
    hu.dataset.target = "u"; svg.appendChild(hu);
    const hv = SVG("circle",{cx:v.x,cy:v.y,r:9,fill:"var(--cyan)",stroke:"#111","stroke-width":1.5,style:"cursor:grab"});
    hv.dataset.target = "v"; svg.appendChild(hv);
    // numeric values: convert pixel offsets to data-units (60 px = 1 unit)
    const ux = (u.x - O.x)/60, uy = -(u.y - O.y)/60;
    const vx = (v.x - O.x)/60, vy = -(v.y - O.y)/60;
    const omega = ux*vy - uy*vx;
    const g = ux*vx + uy*vy;
    if(mode === "omega"){
      out.textContent = `ω(u,v) = u_x·v_y − u_y·v_x = (${ux.toFixed(2)})(${vy.toFixed(2)}) − (${uy.toFixed(2)})(${vx.toFixed(2)}) = ${omega.toFixed(3)}    [signed area]`;
    } else {
      out.textContent = `g(u,v) = u_x·v_x + u_y·v_y = (${ux.toFixed(2)})(${vx.toFixed(2)}) + (${uy.toFixed(2)})(${vy.toFixed(2)}) = ${g.toFixed(3)}    [symmetric, no orientation]`;
    }
  }
  let dragging = null;
  svg.addEventListener("pointerdown", (e) => {
    const t = e.target;
    if(t && t.dataset && t.dataset.target){
      dragging = t.dataset.target;
      svg.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId);
    }
  });
  svg.addEventListener("pointermove", (e) => {
    if(!dragging) return;
    const pt = svg.getBoundingClientRect();
    const sx = (e.clientX - pt.left) * (760 / pt.width);
    const sy = (e.clientY - pt.top) * (320 / pt.height);
    const ref = dragging === "u" ? u : v;
    ref.x = Math.max(220, Math.min(560, sx));
    ref.y = Math.max(40, Math.min(290, sy));
    draw();
  });
  svg.addEventListener("pointerup", () => { dragging = null; });
  bOmega.addEventListener("click", () => { mode = "omega"; bOmega.classList.add("active"); bG.classList.remove("active"); draw(); });
  bG.addEventListener("click", () => { mode = "g"; bG.classList.add("active"); bOmega.classList.remove("active"); draw(); });
  draw();
})();

/* §2 Moser deformation */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("d-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const t = document.getElementById("d-t");
  const tOut = document.getElementById("d-t-out");
  const out = document.getElementById("d-out");
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "<title>Widget · Moser-trick interpolation between two symplectic forms</title>";
    const tt = parseFloat(t.value)/100;
    // grid of cells; at t=1 we shear by S(x,y) = (x + 0.4 y, y * 1.3)
    const cx0 = 380, cy0 = 170;
    const N = 4; // half count of cells each direction
    const cell = 36;
    // background
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:60,y:30,width:640,height:260,fill:"var(--panel2)",stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1,rx:8}));
    // shear matrix: I + tt * A where A = [[0, 0.5],[0, 0.3]]
    const a = 1, b = tt*0.5, c = 0, d = 1 + tt*0.3;
    function map(p,q){ return {x: cx0 + (a*p + b*q)*cell, y: cy0 + (c*p + d*q)*cell}; }
    // grid lines (light)
    for(let i=-N;i<=N;i++){
      const p1 = map(i,-N), p2 = map(i,N);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:p1.x,y1:p1.y,x2:p2.x,y2:p2.y,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
      const q1 = map(-N,i), q2 = map(N,i);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:q1.x,y1:q1.y,x2:q2.x,y2:q2.y,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    }
    // highlight a single cell to track
    const c00 = map(0,0), c10 = map(1,0), c11 = map(1,1), c01 = map(0,1);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("polygon",{
      points:`${c00.x},${c00.y} ${c10.x},${c10.y} ${c11.x},${c11.y} ${c01.x},${c01.y}`,
      fill:"rgba(255,216,102,0.28)", stroke:"var(--yellow)", "stroke-width":2
    }));
    // dp ∧ dq vectors at origin
    drawArrow(svg, [c00.x,c00.y], [c10.x,c10.y], {color:"var(--cyan)", markerId:"d-p", width:2});
    drawArrow(svg, [c00.x,c00.y], [c01.x,c01.y], {color:"var(--pink)", markerId:"d-q", width:2});
    const lp = SVG("text",{x:c10.x+8,y:c10.y+14,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--cyan)","font-style":"italic"}); lp.textContent = "∂/∂p"; svg.appendChild(lp);
    const lq = SVG("text",{x:c01.x+8,y:c01.y+4,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--pink)","font-style":"italic"}); lq.textContent = "∂/∂q"; svg.appendChild(lq);
    // total area is preserved: det(I + tA) up to first order? Actually det = a*d - b*c. Track it.
    const det = a*d - b*c;
    const detSign = det > 0 ? "" : "−";
    tOut.textContent = `t = ${tt.toFixed(2)}`;
    out.textContent = `det of cell deformation = ${det.toFixed(3)}     ω₁ = (1−t) dp∧dq + t · sheared dp∧dq    ·    Moser flow rectifies ω_t back to ω₀ via a symplectomorphism`;
  }
  t.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();

    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:cx+8,y:26,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"})).textContent = "p";
    // gradient field (X_H = (-q, p)) drawn as small ticks
    for(let qq=-4;qq<=4;qq++){
      for(let pp=-3;pp<=3;pp++){
        if(qq===0 && pp===0) continue;
        const px = cx + qq*40, py = cy - pp*40;
        const vx = -qq, vy = pp;
        const len = Math.hypot(vx, vy) || 1;
        const sx = vx/len * 8, sy = -vy/len * 8;
        svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:px-sx/2,y1:py-sy/2,x2:px+sx/2,y2:py+sy/2,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
      }
    }
    // level circles for several energies
    const Eval = parseFloat(E.value)/10;
    const levels = [0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 3.5];
    for(const Ek of levels){
      const r = Math.sqrt(2*Ek)*40;
      const isCurrent = Math.abs(Ek - Eval) < 0.26;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx,cy,r,fill:"none",
        stroke: isCurrent ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--blue)",
        "stroke-width": isCurrent ? 2 : 1,
        opacity: isCurrent ? 1 : 0.55
      }));
    }
    // current orbit dot
    const r = Math.sqrt(2*Eval)*40;
    const ox = cx + r*Math.cos(phase);
    const oy = cy - r*Math.sin(phase);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:ox,cy:oy,r:8,fill:"var(--yellow)",stroke:"#111","stroke-width":1.5}));
    // tangent arrow at the dot
    const tvx = -Math.sin(phase)*30, tvy = -Math.cos(phase)*30;
    drawArrow(svg, [ox,oy], [ox+tvx, oy+tvy], {color:"var(--cyan)", markerId:"h-tan", width:2});
    eOut.textContent = `E = ${Eval.toFixed(2)}`;
    out.textContent = `H = (p² + q²)/2 = ${Eval.toFixed(2)}    ·    X_H = (−q, p)    ·    period 2π    ·    flow: rotation by t   q ↦ q cos t + p sin t`;
  }
  function step(now){
    if(!lastT) lastT = now;
    const dt = (now - lastT)/1000; lastT = now;
    if(playing){
      phase += dt * 1.4;
      draw();
      raf = requestAnimationFrame(step);
    }
  }
  playBtn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    playing = !playing;
    playBtn.textContent = playing ? "pause" : "play";
    if(playing){ lastT = 0; raf = requestAnimationFrame(step); }
    else if(raf){ cancelAnimationFrame(raf); raf = null; }
  });
  E.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();

/* §4 Poisson / commutator correspondence ladder */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("po-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const out = document.getElementById("po-out");
  const rows = [
    {classical:"q (position)", classicalAlt:"p (momentum)", bracket:"{q, p} = 1", quantum:"[q̂, p̂] = iℏ", explain:"Canonical commutation relation — the Heisenberg algebra."},
    {classical:"L_x", classicalAlt:"L_y", bracket:"{L_x, L_y} = L_z", quantum:"[L̂_x, L̂_y] = iℏ L̂_z", explain:"Angular momentum closes under brackets — the so(3) Lie algebra."},
    {classical:"H (energy)", classicalAlt:"f (observable)", bracket:"{H, f} = −df/dt", quantum:"[Ĥ, f̂]/(iℏ) = df̂/dt", explain:"Heisenberg picture: time evolution is a bracket with the Hamiltonian."}
  ];
  let active = -1;
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "<title>Widget · Dirac correspondence between classical and quantum brackets</title>";
    // headers
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:170,y:36,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--cyan)","font-weight":700})).textContent = "Classical";
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:380,y:36,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--mute)"})).textContent = "→  ℏ → 0  ←";
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:600,y:36,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--pink)","font-weight":700})).textContent = "Quantum";
    rows.forEach((row, i) => {
      const y = 70 + i*70;
      const isActive = i === active;
      // classical box
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:50,y:y-22,width:240,height:48,rx:6,
        fill: isActive ? "rgba(125,224,214,0.15)" : "var(--panel2)",
        stroke: isActive ? "var(--cyan)" : "var(--line)", "stroke-width":1.5,
        style:"cursor:pointer"
      })).dataset.row = i;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:170,y:y-4,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--ink)"})).textContent = row.bracket;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:170,y:y+16,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"})).textContent = row.classical + " ,  " + row.classicalAlt;
      // bridge
      const ld = "5 4";
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:294,y1:y,x2:466,y2:y,
        stroke: isActive ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--mute)",
        "stroke-width": isActive ? 2 : 1, "stroke-dasharray":ld
      }));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:380,y:y-6,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":10,fill:"var(--mute)","font-style":"italic"})).textContent = "{·,·} ↔ [·,·]/(iℏ)";
      // quantum box
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:470,y:y-22,width:240,height:48,rx:6,
        fill: isActive ? "rgba(224,122,95,0.15)" : "var(--panel2)",
        stroke: isActive ? "var(--pink)" : "var(--line)", "stroke-width":1.5,
        style:"cursor:pointer"
      })).dataset.row = i;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:590,y:y+4,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--ink)"})).textContent = row.quantum;
    });
    out.textContent = active === -1 ? "click a pair" : rows[active].explain;
  }
  svg.addEventListener("click", (e) => {
    const t = e.target;
    if(t && t.dataset && t.dataset.row !== undefined){
      active = parseInt(t.dataset.row, 10);
      draw();
    }
  });
  draw();
})();

/* §5 Lagrangians of T*S^1 */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("lg-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const out = document.getElementById("lg-out");
  const bZero = document.getElementById("lg-zero");
  const bConst = document.getElementById("lg-const");
  const bFiber = document.getElementById("lg-fiber");
  let mode = "zero";
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "<title>Widget · Lagrangian submanifolds of $T^*S^1$</title>";
    // cylinder as a rectangle [θ ∈ 0..2π] × [p ∈ -2..2]
    const x0 = 100, x1 = 660, y0 = 50, y1 = 270;
    const yp = (p) => y0 + (y1-y0) * (1 - (p+2)/4);
    const xt = (theta) => x0 + (x1-x0) * (theta/(2*Math.PI));
    // background panel
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:x0,y:y0,width:x1-x0,height:y1-y0,fill:"var(--panel2)",stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1,rx:6}));
    // grid: horizontal p-lines and vertical θ-lines
    for(let p=-2;p<=2;p++){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:x0,y1:yp(p),x2:x1,y2:yp(p),stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":0.7,"stroke-dasharray":"3 3"}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:x0-12,y:yp(p)+4,"text-anchor":"end","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"})).textContent = `p=${p}`;
    }
    for(let i=0;i<=4;i++){
      const th = i*Math.PI/2;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:xt(th),y1:y0,x2:xt(th),y2:y1,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":0.7,"stroke-dasharray":"3 3"}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:xt(th),y:y1+18,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"})).textContent =
        i===0?"0":(i===1?"π/2":(i===2?"π":(i===3?"3π/2":"2π")));
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:(x0+x1)/2,y:y1+34,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)","font-style":"italic"})).textContent = "θ ∈ S¹";
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:x0-50,y:(y0+y1)/2,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)","font-style":"italic","transform":`rotate(-90 ${x0-50} ${(y0+y1)/2})`})).textContent = "p (cotangent)";
    // Lagrangians
    let label = "", desc = "";
    if(mode === "zero"){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:x0,y1:yp(0),x2:x1,y2:yp(0),stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":3}));
      label = "zero section";
      desc = "L = {p = 0} ⊂ T*S¹    ·    α = 0    ·    closed (trivially)    ·    Lagrangian: dim 1 = ½ dim T*S¹, ω|_L = 0";
    } else if(mode === "const"){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:x0,y1:yp(1),x2:x1,y2:yp(1),stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":3}));
      label = "graph of α = 1 dθ";
      desc = "L = {p = 1}    ·    α = 1·dθ is closed (constant)    ·    horizontal section, parallel to zero section    ·    integer p ∈ ℤ corresponds to degree of holonomy";
    } else {
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:xt(Math.PI/2),y1:y0,x2:xt(Math.PI/2),y2:y1,stroke:"var(--pink)","stroke-width":3}));
      label = "cotangent fiber at θ = π/2";
      desc = "L = {θ = π/2} = T*_{π/2} S¹    ·    vertical fiber    ·    Lagrangian: dim 1, ω = dθ∧dp restricts to 0 since dθ vanishes    ·    transversal to zero section at one point";
    }
    out.textContent = desc;
  }
  bZero.addEventListener("click", () => { mode = "zero"; bZero.classList.add("active"); bConst.classList.remove("active"); bFiber.classList.remove("active"); draw(); });
  bConst.addEventListener("click", () => { mode = "const"; bConst.classList.add("active"); bZero.classList.remove("active"); bFiber.classList.remove("active"); draw(); });
  bFiber.addEventListener("click", () => { mode = "fiber"; bFiber.classList.add("active"); bZero.classList.remove("active"); bConst.classList.remove("active"); draw(); });
  draw();
})();

/* §6 Lagrangian Floer intersections on T^2 */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("fl-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const shift = document.getElementById("fl-shift");
  const sOut = document.getElementById("fl-shift-out");
  const out = document.getElementById("fl-out");
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "<title>Widget · Lagrangian Floer intersections on a torus</title>";
    const x0 = 180, x1 = 580, y0 = 30, y1 = 290;
    // torus fundamental domain
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:x0,y:y0,width:x1-x0,height:y1-y0,fill:"var(--panel2)",stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1,rx:6}));
    // identify edges
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:(x0+x1)/2,y:y0-8,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)","font-style":"italic"})).textContent = "T² = ℝ²/ℤ²  (sides identified)";
    // L_0: y = 0.5 (red horizontal)
    const yL0 = y0 + (y1-y0) * 0.5;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:x0,y1:yL0,x2:x1,y2:yL0,stroke:"var(--pink)","stroke-width":3}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:x1+8,y:yL0+4,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--pink)","font-style":"italic"})).textContent = "L₀";
    // L_1: deformed vertical curve x = 0.5 + a·cos(2π y) (Hamiltonian-isotopic)
    const a = parseFloat(shift.value)/100 * 0.18; // up to ~0.18 amplitude
    const samples = 80;
    let pts = "";
    for(let i=0;i<=samples;i++){
      const yy = i/samples; // y in [0,1]
      const xx = 0.5 + a * Math.sin(2*Math.PI*yy);
      const sx = x0 + (x1-x0)*xx, sy = y0 + (y1-y0)*yy;
      pts += `${sx},${sy} `;
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("polyline",{points:pts.trim(),fill:"none",stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":3}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:x0 + (x1-x0)*0.5 + 14,y:y0+18,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--cyan)","font-style":"italic"})).textContent = "L₁";
    // intersections: solve y such that L_1 crosses y = 0.5, i.e. yy = 0.5 always → x = 0.5
    // but we want intersections of curve with y = 0.5 in cylindrical sense — since L_0 wraps the whole row at yy=0.5,
    // intersections are at all yy where the curve touches yy = 0.5: just yy = 0.5 → one point at (0.5, 0.5).
    // For Lagrangian Floer on T^2 with L_0 = horizontal circle and L_1 = vertical circle, generic intersection is 1 point.
    // To show the lower bound = |H_*(S^1)| = 2, draw both fundamental-domain crossings (the same point under T^2 identification, but two preimages displayed).
    // Display at (0.5, 0.5) only:
    const ix = x0 + (x1-x0)*0.5, iy = yL0;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:ix,cy:iy,r:9,fill:"var(--yellow)",stroke:"#111","stroke-width":1.5}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:ix+14,y:iy-10,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--yellow)"})).textContent = "L₀ ∩ L₁";
    // Show edge identifications by tagging them
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:x0-12,y:(y0+y1)/2,"text-anchor":"end","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"})).textContent = "x ≡ x+1";
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:(x0+x1)/2,y:y1+18,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"})).textContent = "y ≡ y+1";
    sOut.textContent = `θ = ${(parseFloat(shift.value)/100).toFixed(2)}`;
    const tt = parseFloat(shift.value)/100;
    out.textContent = `|L₀ ∩ L₁| = 1 in this fundamental domain  ·  amplitude a = ${a.toFixed(3)}  ·  Hamiltonian isotopy of L₁ never decreases |L₀ ∩ L₁| below the Floer rank — generically the rank predicts a positive lower bound coming from H_*(L₀; ℤ/2)`;
  }
  shift.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* sidebar TOC builder + active-section highlighting */
(function(){
  function build(){
    const aside = document.querySelector('aside.sidetoc');
    const topnav = document.querySelector('nav.toc');
    if(!aside || !topnav) return;
    const srcs = [...topnav.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]')];
    if(!srcs.length) return;
    const title = document.createElement('div');
    title.className = 'sidetoc-title';
    title.textContent = 'On this page';
    aside.appendChild(title);
    const ol = document.createElement('ol');
    srcs.forEach(src => {
      const li = document.createElement('li');
      const a = document.createElement('a');
      a.href = src.getAttribute('href');
      a.textContent = src.textContent.replace(/\u00a0/g,' ').trim();
      li.appendChild(a); ol.appendChild(li);
    });
    aside.appendChild(ol);
    const links = [...aside.querySelectorAll('a')];
    const sections = [];
    links.forEach(a => {
      const id = a.getAttribute('href').slice(1);
      const s = document.getElementById(id);
      if(s) sections.push({a, s});
    });
    if(!sections.length) return;
    function update(){
      const threshold = 140;
      let activeLink = sections[0].a;
      for(const {a,s} of sections){
        if(s.getBoundingClientRect().top <= threshold) activeLink = a;
      }
      links.forEach(x => x.classList.remove('active'));
      activeLink.classList.add('active');
    }
    update();
    window.addEventListener('scroll', update, {passive:true});
    window.addEventListener('resize', update);
  }
  if(document.readyState === 'loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', build);
  else build();
})();
</script>
